content: Your next monthly bill will be {{currency}} starting from {{date}}

comment.text: Displays the date and currency amount for the user's next monthly bill. This text appears within a UI element, likely a button or informational area, to inform users about their upcoming bill amount and when it will be charged. The currency and date are dynamically inserted into the text.

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The German translation implies the bill itself 'starts' with a currency, which is semantically incorrect and awkward. It suggests the bill is a process that begins, rather than stating the amount of the bill.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Ihre nächste monatliche Rechnung beträgt ab {{date}} {{currency}}

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The word 'λογοκρισίωση' means 'censorship', whereas the English text refers to a 'bill' (λογαριασμός).
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Η επόμενη μηνιαία χρέωσή σας θα είναι {{currency}} ξεκινώντας από {{date}}

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Korean translation incorrectly implies that the bill 'starts with' a currency amount ({{currency}}로 시작됩니다), which is semantically awkward and potentially misleading. In the context of a bill, it should state the amount of the bill.
The word 'Jūras' means 'Sea' in Latvian. It appears to be an accidental insertion or a hallucination, making the sentence 'Sea's next monthly bill...', which is nonsensical.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Tālākais nākamais mēneša rēķins būs {{currency}} no {{date}}

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Romanian translation changes the sentence structure in a way that implies the bill 'starts' from a date and 'will be' a currency, which is semantically awkward and slightly deviates from the English meaning of 'the bill will be [amount] starting from [date]'. More importantly, the English structure implies the amount is the subject of the 'will be' clause, whereas the Romanian version separates them awkwardly.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Următoarea dumneavoastră factură lunară va fi de {{currency}} începând cu {{date}}

ai_spell_check_issues.description: Grammatical gender mismatch: 'račun' (bill) is masculine in Slovenian, but the adjective 'veljavna' is feminine. This changes the grammatical correctness of the sentence structure.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Vaš naslednji mesečni račun bo {{currency}} in bo veljavna od {{date}}
